Title: Differential regulation of closely related R2R3-MYB transcription factors controls flavonol accumulation in different parts of the Arabidopsis thaliana seedling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl-root transition zone, and roots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized phenylpropanoid metabolites; flavonols are implicated in light/gravity responses and bud outgrowth control as discussed in the paper
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with production of distinct flavonol glycosides; transcript levels are strongly reduced in the myb11 myb12 myb111 triple mutant
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in wild-type roots of 5-dag seedlings; strongly reduced in roots of the myb11 myb12 myb111 triple mutant
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified by ATH1 transcriptome analysis as strongly downregulated in roots of the myb11 myb12 myb111 triple mutant. 2. qPCR confirmed strong reduction of transcript abundance in the triple mutant. 3. Its promoter was strongly activated by MYB11, MYB12, and MYB111 in Arabidopsis protoplast co-transfection assays, supporting it as a direct/primary SG7 MYB target linked to flavonol glycoside production.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings grown on 0.5× MS, 1.5% sucrose, vertical plates, 16 h light at 22°C
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ATH1 microarray screening; qPCR; promoter::GUS co-transfection assay in At7 protoplasts
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a glycosyltransferase likely involved in glycosylation of specific flavonols or flavonol-glycosides, but exact biochemical reaction was not determined in this paper.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl-root transition zone, and roots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized phenylpropanoid metabolites; flavonols are implicated in light/gravity responses and bud outgrowth control as discussed in the paper
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with production of flavonol glycosides; transcript abundance is strongly reduced in the myb11 myb12 myb111 triple mutant
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in wild-type roots of 5-dag seedlings; strongly reduced in roots of the myb11 myb12 myb111 triple mutant
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified by transcriptome profiling as an SG7 MYB-responsive glycosyltransferase gene. 2. qPCR validated strong downregulation in the SG7 triple mutant. 3. Its promoter was highly responsive to MYB11, MYB12, and MYB111 in protoplast assays, linking it to regulation of flavonol glycoside formation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings grown on 0.5× MS, 1.5% sucrose, vertical plates, 16 h light at 22°C
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ATH1 microarray screening; qPCR; promoter::GUS co-transfection assay in At7 protoplasts
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously shown in vitro to form glucose esters of sinapic acid and other cinnamic acids; this paper infers involvement in phenylpropanoid/flavonol glycoside metabolism via transcriptional regulation, not direct in planta enzyme proof.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shared early flavonoid pathway feeding flavonol and anthocyanin branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for pathway entry; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl-root transition zone, and roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized phenylpropanoid metabolites; flavonols are implicated in light/gravity responses and bud outgrowth control as discussed in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for flavonol and anthocyanin formation; promoter activated by MYB11, MYB12 and MYB111, and transcript is strongly reduced in SG7 triple-mutant roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in 5-dag seedling roots; reduced in roots of the myb11 myb12 myb111 triple mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS promoter was strongly transactivated by MYB11, MYB12 and MYB111 in protoplasts. 2. CHS transcript was strongly reduced in roots of the SG7 triple mutant in microarray data. 3. Despite SG7 loss, anthocyanins still accumulated, indicating CHS is also controlled by other factors in anthocyanin-producing tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings grown on 0.5× MS, 1.5% sucrose, vertical plates, 16 h light at 22°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter co-transfection assay; ATH1 transcriptome profiling; metabolite analysis using tt4 mutant comparison
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as control target gene; chs/tt4 mutant lacks flavonoids entirely and served as reference for flavonol absence.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl-root transition zone, and roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive SG7 target gene required for formation of the flavonol aglycon
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in 5-dag seedling roots; reduced in roots of the SG7 triple mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI promoter responded to MYB11, MYB12 and MYB111 in protoplast co-transfection assays. 2. CHI transcript abundance was reduced in roots of the SG7 triple mutant in microarray analysis. 3. Identified as one of the common flavonol-biosynthetic target genes of the three SG7 MYBs.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings on sucrose-containing MS medium under long-day light
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter co-transfection assay; ATH1 transcriptome profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl-root transition zone, and roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive SG7 target gene contributing to flavonol aglycon formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots of 5-dag seedlings; reduced in roots of the SG7 triple mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H promoter was activated by MYB11, MYB12 and MYB111 in protoplast assays. 2. F3H transcript was reduced in SG7 triple-mutant roots in transcriptome analysis. 3. F3H is one of the four directly responsive flavonol biosynthesis genes required for aglycon formation according to the authors.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings under 16 h light on vertical MS plates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter co-transfection assay; ATH1 transcriptome profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for flavonol branch in this study context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl-root transition zone, and roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive SG7 target gene required for flavonol formation; loss of SG7 activity abolishes flavonol accumulation in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots of 5-dag seedlings; markedly reduced in SG7 triple-mutant roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS1 promoter was activated by MYB11, MYB12 and MYB111 in co-transfection assays. 2. FLS1 transcript was strongly reduced in roots of the SG7 triple mutant. 3. Because FLS activity is required for flavonol biosynthesis, SG7 regulation of FLS1 is central to the flavonol-specific phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings under standard growth conditions described in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter co-transfection assay; ATH1 transcriptome profiling; metabolite profiling of SG7 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: General phenylpropanoid pathway feeding flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedling cotyledons, hypocotyl-root transition zone, and roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of the broader PFG/SG7 regulon that supports precursor supply for flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots of 5-dag seedlings; reduced in SG7 triple-mutant roots in transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL3 was identified by ATH1 screening as downregulated in roots of the SG7 triple mutant. 2. This indicates that SG7 MYBs regulate not only flavonol branch genes but also an upstream general phenylpropanoid pathway gene. 3. The paper uses 4CL3 as evidence that the regulon extends into precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedling roots under standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in transcriptomics but not directly tested by promoter transactivation in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: primarily roots and hypocotyl-root transition zone of seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonols are specialized phenylpropanoids implicated in light/gravity responses and bud outgrowth control as discussed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in roots of 5-dag seedlings; low in cotyledons
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter analyzed by ProMYB12:GUS; endogenous promoter, tissue-specific
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Spatial developmental cues in seedlings; specific upstream regulators not identified
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonol biosynthesis and flavonol glycoside production
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; CHI; F3H; FLS1; UGT91A1; UGT84A1; At5g62210; 4CL3; F3'H (candidate)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates promoters of CHS, CHI, F3H, FLS1, UGT91A1, UGT84A1 and At5g62210; transcriptome data support positive regulation of 4CL3 and F3'H
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible metabolic feedback discussed for F3'H regulation, but not established for MYB12 itself
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major spatial regulator controlling flavonol biosynthesis mainly in roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol accumulation mainly in roots; myb12 mutants show drastically reduced flavonols in the root and hypocotyl-root transition zone
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB12 shares target specificity with MYB11 and MYB111 for key flavonol biosynthetic genes. 2. It is predominantly expressed in roots and drives root flavonol accumulation. 3. Together with MYB11 and MYB111, it is essential for seedling flavonol production; triple mutants lack flavonols but still accumulate anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings grown on 0.5× MS, 1.5% sucrose, vertical plates, 16 h light at 22°C
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; double/triple mutant phenotyping; qPCR; promoter:GUS; protoplast transactivation; HPLC/HPTLC metabolite profiling; DPBA staining
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces flavonols in specific seedling organs; triple loss slightly increases anthocyanins due to precursor redirection
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Renamed PFG1 (PRODUCTION OF FLAVONOL GLYCOSIDES1). The paper emphasizes additive action with MYB11 and MYB111 due to differential spatial expression.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: apical meristem region, cotyledon apex, hypocotyl-root transition, lateral root origin, root tip
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonols are specialized phenylpropanoids implicated in developmental and environmental responses
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low transcript levels across seedling samples; promoter activity in apical meristem, primary leaves, cotyledon apex, hypocotyl-root transition, lateral root origin, root tip and vascular tissue of lateral roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter analyzed by ProMYB11:GUS; endogenous promoter, tissue-specific
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Spatial developmental cues; not further defined
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonol biosynthesis and flavonol glycoside production
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; CHI; F3H; FLS1; UGT91A1; UGT84A1; At5g62210
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates multiple flavonol biosynthetic and glycosyltransferase promoters, generally weaker than MYB111 and MYB12 in assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Additive spatial contributor to flavonol production in defined seedling domains rather than dominant global regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports flavonol accumulation in defined domains; single myb11 mutant shows little obvious effect, but contributes additively with MYB12 and MYB111
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB11 is a flavonol-specific SG7 regulator with similar target specificity to MYB12 and MYB111. 2. Its expression is low but spatially restricted to several seedling domains. 3. Loss of MYB11 alone has little effect, but combined loss with the other SG7 MYBs contributes to complete flavonol deficiency in seedlings.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings grown on 0.5× MS, 1.5% sucrose, vertical plates, 16 h light at 22°C
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; qPCR; promoter:GUS; protoplast transactivation; metabolite profiling; DPBA staining
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single knockout had no visible phenotype under greenhouse conditions; redundancy may limit standalone engineering value
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Renamed PFG2. Null allele carried by SALK077068 with premature stop codon; transactivation generally weaker than MYB111.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: primarily cotyledons and primary leaves of seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonols are specialized phenylpropanoids implicated in developmental responses
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in cotyledons and primary leaves of 5-dag seedlings; hardly detectable in roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter analyzed by ProMYB111:GUS; endogenous promoter, tissue-specific
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Spatial developmental cues; not further defined
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonol biosynthesis and flavonol glycoside production
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; CHI; F3H; FLS1; UGT91A1; UGT84A1; At5g62210
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Strongly activates promoters of key flavonol biosynthetic genes and candidate glycosyltransferase genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major spatial regulator controlling cotyledon flavonol accumulation and specific glycoside patterns such as kaempferol-rhamnoside-rhamnoside
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol accumulation primarily in cotyledons; myb111 mutants lack flavonols in cotyledons
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB111 has similar target specificity to MYB11 and MYB12 but is preferentially expressed in cotyledons. 2. It shows strong transactivation capacity on CHS and other flavonol gene promoters. 3. MYB111 is a principal regulator of cotyledon flavonol accumulation and specific derivative patterns, acting additively with MYB12 and MYB11.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old seedlings grown on 0.5× MS, 1.5% sucrose, vertical plates, 16 h light at 22°C
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; qPCR; promoter:GUS; protoplast transactivation; HPLC/HPTLC metabolite profiling; DPBA staining
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss decreases cotyledon flavonols; combined loss with other SG7 MYBs abolishes flavonols and slightly elevates anthocyanins by flux redirection
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix ATH1 microarray, ArrayExpress accession E-ATMX-5
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Renamed PFG3. Promoter activity restricted to cotyledons and primary leaves; stronger transactivation than MYB11 in assays.
